meta.description: Single source of truth for Sublemonable v1.0 and v1.5 specifications. v2.0 spec lives in Claude/sublemonable-v2-blueprint.json. Design preview in Claude/sublemonable-preview.html.

project.name: Sublemonable
project.description: Zero-knowledge, end-to-end encrypted ephemeral messaging app for browser, iOS, Android, and Linux desktop

security.philosophy: Zero-knowledge architecture. The server never sees, stores, or logs plaintext message content under any circumstances.

protected_against: Server compromise — messages encrypted before leaving device
Forward secrecy breach — Double Ratchet key rotation per message
Screenshot leaks — platform-specific prevention and detection
Metadata surveillance — minimal metadata, optional Tor routing
Replay attacks — message nonces and timestamp validation
Brute force — Argon2id key derivation for all passwords
out_of_scope: Full OS-level screenshot prevention on browser

libraries.linux: Tauri v2 + secret-service Rust crate (libsecret / GNOME Keyring / KWallet)

key_types.identity_key: Curve25519 long-term keypair, generated on device, never leaves device
key_types.signed_prekey: Curve25519, rotated every 7 days, signed by identity key
key_types.one_time_prekeys: Curve25519, batch of 100 uploaded to server (public keys only), consumed once
key_types.session_keys: Derived via X3DH on first message, Double Ratchet thereafter
key_types.message_keys: AES-256-GCM, derived per message, discarded after use

key_storage.web: IndexedDB encrypted with AES-256-GCM, master key derived from passphrase via Argon2id
key_storage.android: Android Keystore System, hardware-backed where available
key_storage.linux: Secret Service API (GNOME Keyring / KWallet) via secret-service Rust crate. File fallback ($XDG_DATA_HOME/sublemonable/vault.bin) if no daemon available. Vault blob is Argon2id+AES-256-GCM encrypted by packages/crypto before reaching Rust storage layer.

certificate_pinning.ios: NSURLSession with pinned certificate SHA-256 hash
certificate_pinning.web: TLS 1.3 + HSTS (max-age=63072000; preload). True certificate pinning is not available in browser environments — HPKP was removed from all major browsers and Service Workers cannot access the TLS certificate chain. Web transport relies on CA chain validation + HSTS preload. Users requiring hard pinning should use the native iOS or Android clients.

tor.method: Orbot integration on mobile, .onion address for browser via Tor Browser

what_is_stored: Encrypted message envelopes (blob only, no metadata)
Delivery receipts (hash of message ID only)

message_persistence.lifetime: Messages deleted from server immediately upon delivery confirmation
message_persistence.no_delivery_action: Message purged after TTL, sender notified

api.style: REST for auth/registration + WebSocket for messaging
api.authentication: JWT (RS256, 15min expiry) + refresh token (7 days, rotated on use)

endpoints.POST /api/v1/register: Create account — accepts public identity key + prekeys only
endpoints.GET /api/v1/users/:id/prekey: Fetch one-time prekey bundle for X3DH
endpoints.POST /api/v1/prekeys: Upload new batch of one-time prekeys
endpoints.GET /api/v1/prekeys/count: Check prekey stock (triggers client to upload more)
endpoints.WS /ws: Authenticated WebSocket for real-time message delivery
endpoints.DELETE /api/v1/account: Full account deletion — purges all data

client_to_server: message.burn — request early destruction of a message

logging.application_logs: Errors and system events only — no request body logging, no user-identifiable data

crypto.description: Shared crypto primitives used by web client. Wraps libsodium.js.

protocol.description: Shared message format, types, and serialization (used by web + server TS types)

message_envelope_schema.ephemeral_key: base64 Curve25519 public key (X3DH only, null after)
message_envelope_schema.ttl_seconds: number | null — null means no self-destruct

ui.description: Shared React Native + React component library
components: MessageBubble — renders with blur overlay when capture detected

web.key_storage: IndexedDB via idb library, AES-256-GCM encrypted

pwa.offline_support: Service Worker — queues outbound messages, caches UI only

screenshot_protection.action: Apply CSS filter: blur(24px) + grayscale(1) to #messages-container

watermark.method: Canvas steganography — encode recipient_id + timestamp into message background
watermark.purpose: Leak attribution — if screenshot shared, watermark identifies recipient

features: E2EE messaging via Signal Protocol
Ephemeral messages with configurable TTL
Burn-on-read messages
Blur-on-screenshot-attempt
Invisible watermarking
No clipboard access to message text (CSS user-select: none)
Right-click disabled on message content
DevTools open detection with session warning
Contact key fingerprint verification
Passphrase-protected local key store

ios.key_storage: iOS Keychain + Secure Enclave for identity key

screen_recording_detection.action: Immediately overlay UIVisualEffectView (blur) over message list

screenshot_detection.action: Show warning banner + log event locally
screenshot_detection.note: Fires after screenshot taken — cannot prevent, only react

screenshot_protection.secure_text_fields: All message text rendered in custom UIView with isSecureTextEntry-style protection where possible

features: E2EE messaging via libsignal-client
Ephemeral messages with local + server TTL sync
Burn-on-read with animation
Screenshot detection with blur overlay
Certificate pinning via NSURLSession
Biometric authentication (Face ID / Touch ID)
Background app blur (UIApplicationWillResignActiveNotification)
No message preview in iOS notification center
Notification content extension — shows 'New Message' only, no content
Jailbreak detection (basic heuristics)
Optional Tor via Orbot URL scheme integration

android.key_storage: Android Keystore System (hardware-backed on supported devices)

screenshot_protection.scope: Applied to all Activities containing message content
screenshot_protection.effect: OS-level hard block — screenshots and screen recording show black screen
screenshot_protection.note: Strongest protection of all three platforms

features: E2EE messaging via libsignal-client
FLAG_SECURE — hard OS-level screenshot block
Ephemeral messages with TTL
Burn-on-read
Certificate pinning via OkHttp CertificatePinner
Biometric authentication
EncryptedSharedPreferences for all local data
No notification content in lock screen
Root detection (basic heuristics)
Optional Tor via Orbot intent integration
App masking — ability to disguise app icon

images.encryption: Encrypted as binary blob using same Signal Protocol envelope
images.storage: Never stored on server. Client-to-client direct encrypted transfer via relay

burn_on_read.description: Message destroyed on all devices immediately after first open

typing_indicators.note: Typing events sent as encrypted signals, not plaintext

read_receipts.implementation: Encrypted signal — server never knows read status

group_messaging.note: Phase 2 — requires Sender Keys protocol extension

accounts.username: Optional display name, not used for routing
accounts.routing_id: UUID — never exposed to other users directly
accounts.discovery: QR code exchange or direct link (no phone number, no email required)

contact_verification.method: Safety Number comparison (SHA-512 fingerprint of both identity keys)

platform_warning_system.description: Informs users when their contact is on a platform with reduced screenshot protection. UI layer only — no changes to crypto or server message architecture.
platform_warning_system.purpose: Browser clients cannot block screenshots at the OS level. Contacts on native apps deserve to know this so they can make informed decisions about what to share.

platform_tracking.where: Session metadata — not message content. E2EE is not affected.
platform_tracking.server_stores: Platform string per active session — treated as non-sensitive session metadata
platform_tracking.passed_to_contact: Via encrypted contact/conversation info signal — not embedded in message envelopes
platform_tracking.real_time_updates: If contact switches platforms mid-conversation (e.g. opens web client while mobile is active), warning updates in real time via WebSocket event

warning_display.location: Persistent badge in conversation header, below contact name
warning_display.copy_browser_contact: 🌐 Messaging via browser — screenshot protection unavailable
warning_display.copy_own_browser: 🌐 You're on browser — your messages can be screenshotted
warning_display.dismiss_scope: Per-conversation — dismissing on one conversation does not affect others
warning_display.re_shown: Re-shown if contact switches to browser after being dismissed
warning_display.color: Lemon yellow warning tone — not red, not alarming, informative

who_sees_warning.native_app_user: Sees warning when their contact is on browser
who_sees_warning.browser_user: Sees warning about their own limitations — honest and upfront
who_sees_warning.both_on_browser: Single shared warning shown to both parties

non_intrusive_design.no_modal: Never blocks the UI with a modal or forced acknowledgement
non_intrusive_design.no_alarm: Warning tone is informative not alarming — matches lemon yellow brand
non_intrusive_design.one_time_per_session: After dismissal, only re-appears if platform changes

privacy_view_mode.description: Physical shoulder-surfing defense. Message content blurred by default, revealed only while actively interacting. Captures the blurred state on browser screenshot attempts.
privacy_view_mode.purpose: Protects against someone physically looking at your screen in public — on a train, in a cafe, over your shoulder.

scope.global_toggle: Settings → Privacy → Privacy View — applies to all conversations
scope.per_conversation: Long press conversation → Enable Privacy View — applies to that conversation only
scope.conversation_list: When privacy view active, conversation list previews also blurred — no message content visible on main screen

reveal_modes.description: User selects preferred reveal interaction in Settings → Privacy → Privacy View → Reveal Mode

hold_to_reveal.name: Hold to reveal
hold_to_reveal.description: Content blurred by default. Hold finger/mouse on message area to reveal. Release to re-blur. Most secure.
hold_to_reveal.best_for: High-risk environments, public transport, shared spaces

tap_timed.name: Tap to reveal (timed)
tap_timed.description: Tap to reveal for configurable duration (5s / 10s / 30s), then auto-re-blurs

tap_toggle.name: Tap to toggle
tap_toggle.description: Tap once to reveal, tap again to blur. Stays revealed until tapped again.
tap_toggle.best_for: Low-risk environments where user wants occasional protection

visual_design.blur_style: Frosted lemon-citrus texture overlay — not a generic grey blur
visual_design.texture: Semi-transparent lemon-yellow frosted glass effect — CSS backdrop-filter: blur(20px) with lemon tint rgba(245,230,66,0.08)
visual_design.intentional_look: Looks like a deliberate design choice, not a broken UI — on-brand and intentional
visual_design.bubble_shape_preserved: Message bubble shapes and layout remain visible — only text content is obscured
visual_design.reveal_animation: Smooth 150ms blur-out on reveal, 200ms blur-in on re-blur — fast enough to feel responsive

screenshot_behavior.android: FLAG_SECURE still active — screenshot shows black screen regardless of privacy view state
screenshot_behavior.ios: Screenshot detection triggers blur overlay. If privacy view already active, screenshot captures blurred state — partial mitigation.
screenshot_behavior.browser: Screenshots capture whatever is currently on screen. If privacy view active and content blurred, screenshot captures blurred state. This is the best mitigation available on browser.

notification_behavior.when_privacy_view_active: Push notifications show sender name only — no message preview regardless of OS notification settings
notification_behavior.lock_screen: No message content on lock screen when privacy view enabled for that conversation

platform_implementation.web: CSS backdrop-filter on message container. Hold event via mousedown/touchstart + mouseup/touchend. Privacy view state stored in encrypted local settings.
platform_implementation.ios: SwiftUI blur modifier with custom lemon tint. Long press gesture recognizer for hold-to-reveal. UINotificationFeedbackGenerator on reveal for tactile confirmation.
platform_implementation.android: Jetpack Compose BlurMaskFilter with lemon tint overlay. pointerInput for hold detection. HapticFeedbackType.LongPress on reveal.

indicator.active_indicator: Small frosted lemon slice icon in conversation header when privacy view active for that conversation
indicator.global_indicator: Lemon slice icon in app status bar area when global privacy view enabled

phase_1.name: Core Encrypted Messaging
deliverables: Server-side message deletion on delivery ack

phase_3_5.name: Linux Desktop App
deliverables: apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/keystore.rs — libsecret + file fallback vault storage
apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/screenshot.rs — best-effort focus-loss blur signal (no OS-level hard block exists on Linux; Wayland and X11 both fall back to the browser-style blur overlay)
apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/tor.rs — SOCKS5 proxy config and connectivity check
apps/web/src/lib/storage.ts — conditional Tauri invoke routing for vault ops
docs/SETUP.md — Linux desktop prerequisites and build instructions
apps/desktop/README.md — installation, Tor routing, key storage docs

testing.unit: Go test (server), Vitest (web), XCTest (iOS), JUnit (Android), cargo test (desktop Rust)

fable_instructions.note: Instructions for Claude Code / Fable to build this project end-to-end
fable_instructions.start_here: Read this entire JSON before writing a single line of code
order: 1. Scaffold monorepo with pnpm workspaces and all root directories
2. Write all open source root files: LICENSE AGPL-3.0 full text, README.md, SECURITY.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, AUDIT.md, CHANGELOG.md
3. Write GitHub templates: .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md, feature_request.md, PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md
4. Write all docs: docs/SECURITY_MODEL.md, docs/SETUP.md, docs/SELF_HOSTING.md
5. Build packages/protocol — TypeScript types and message schemas
6. Build packages/crypto — libsodium.js wrappers for all key operations
7. Build packages/ui — LemonSlice SVG animated component FIRST, then all other components from design_system.components
8. Build server in Go — database schema sqlc, WebSocket hub, REST endpoints, JWT auth
9. Build apps/web — React + Vite, integrate packages/crypto + packages/ui, full chat UI with screenshot protection and invisible watermark
10. Build apps/android — Kotlin + Compose, FLAG_SECURE, libsignal-client, OkHttp pinning
11. Build apps/ios — SwiftUI, libsignal-client, Keychain, capture detection overlay

11b. Build apps/desktop — Tauri v2 scaffold, Rust keystore/screenshot/tor modules, tauri.conf.json with .deb+.AppImage+.rpm bundle targets, platform detection in packages/crypto, conditional storage routing in apps/web
12. Build website — Next.js 14 + Tailwind + Framer Motion marketing site for sublemonable.com per marketing_site spec
13. Write docker-compose.yml + docker-compose.tor.yml
14. Write .github/workflows/ci.yml GitHub Actions CI pipeline
15. Final pass: verify every file in open_source.root_files_to_generate exists and is complete
critical_rules: NEVER log message content anywhere server or client
NEVER store plaintext keys always encrypted at rest
NEVER skip nonce generation every encrypted message needs a unique nonce
NEVER reuse keys one-time prekeys are single-use by design
NEVER add analytics crash reporting or telemetry of any kind
NEVER use white backgrounds in UI minimum dark value is #1A1800
NEVER use blue for interactive elements lemon yellow #F5E642 owns all interactivity
ALWAYS delete server-side message immediately after delivery ack
ALWAYS apply FLAG_SECURE on every Android Activity with message content
ALWAYS use parameterized SQL queries no string concatenation
ALWAYS validate JWT on every authenticated endpoint
ALWAYS rotate refresh tokens on use
ALWAYS apply AGPL-3.0 license header comment to every source file
Every loading state uses the LemonSlice spinner from packages/ui no other loaders
The burn animation uses particle dissolve upward not an opacity fade
Screenshot blur triggers in 120ms max it must feel jarring and protective
Key fingerprints always render in JetBrains Mono
Notification content is always New message never preview message content
fable_instructions.design_reference: See design_system, marketing_site, and fable_ui_instructions keys in this file for full UI/UX spec. Build the LemonSlice SVG component in packages/ui FIRST before any screen work. It is used in logo, loader, burn timer, security badge, and send button.

open_source.license_rationale: Anyone running a modified Sublemonable as a service must open source their changes. Prevents silent commercial forks. Same license used by Signal.

LICENSE.note: Fetch full AGPL-3.0 text from https://www.gnu.org/licenses/agpl-3.0.txt and write verbatim

sections: Hero — lemon slice logo + tagline: Nothing lasts. That's the point.
What is Sublemonable — 2 paragraph plain-language description
Security model — bullet summary linking to docs/SECURITY_MODEL.md
Features — E2EE, burn-on-read, screenshot protection, no phone number required

SECURITY.md.purpose: Responsible disclosure policy for security researchers

sections.title: Reporting a vulnerability
sections.content: Do NOT open a public issue. Use GitHub private security advisories.

sections.title: What to include
sections.content: Vulnerability description, reproduction steps, potential impact, suggested fix if any

sections.title: Response timeline
sections.content: Acknowledgement within 48 hours. Fix target within 90 days of confirmed vulnerability.

sections.title: Scope in scope
sections.content: server, web app, iOS app, Android app, packages/crypto, packages/protocol

sections.title: Recognition
sections.content: Valid disclosures credited in AUDIT.md and release notes unless researcher prefers anonymity

sections.title: Safe harbor
sections.content: Good-faith research under this policy is authorized. We will not pursue legal action against researchers who follow it.

sections.title: Before contributing
sections.content: Read docs/SECURITY_MODEL.md. All contributions must maintain zero-knowledge architecture.

sections.title: Hard rules never do this
items: Add server-side message logging of any kind
Add analytics, telemetry, or crash reporting
Change cryptography without a detailed explanation reviewed by a maintainer

sections.title: PR process
items: Open PR with clear description of what and why

AUDIT.md.initial_content: No audits conducted yet. This file updates as audits complete. Contact us via SECURITY.md if you want to conduct an audit.

docs/SECURITY_MODEL.md.purpose: Full technical security model for users and auditors
sections: Architecture overview with zero knowledge diagram
Key generation and storage per platform
What the server stores and provably cannot store
Transport security TLS 1.3 and certificate pinning
Threat model protected and out of scope

design_system.name: Sublemonable Design System
design_system.philosophy: Secure doesn't have to feel cold. Sublemonable is sharp, bright, and alive — like biting into a lemon. Every screen should feel like privacy is a feature you want, not a compromise you made.

display.usage: App name, hero headlines, section titles

signature_element.name: The Lemon Slice
signature_element.description: A segmented circle that appears as the logo mark, loading indicator, burn timer, and security badge. Drawn with SVG — segments fill with lemon-yellow as encryption handshake completes, as a message timer counts down, or as security level increases. It's the heartbeat of the app — every moment of waiting, protecting, or destroying is shown through this one motif.
variants: burn_timer — segments extinguish as TTL counts down, final segment triggers destruction animation
security_indicator — segments fill based on verification level (0-8)
send_button — holds the lemon slice, pulses on press

burn_on_read.indicator: Small flame icon on bubble corner, lemon-orange color
burn_on_read.on_open: Bubble catches fire animation — particles dissolve upward, text fades, bubble shrinks to nothing over 600ms

timed.indicator: Mini lemon-slice burn timer in corner of bubble
timed.warning_pulse: Lemon glow pulses when <10% time remains

screenshot_blur.web: filter: blur(24px) saturate(0) — applied instantly on visibilitychange/blur
screenshot_blur.description: Entire message list blurs — layout remains visible, content does not

burn_timer_ring.description: The lemon slice SVG used as countdown indicator on timed messages
burn_timer_ring.animation: Each segment fades out sequentially as TTL expires. Final segment triggers destruction animation.

preview_text.text: Always shows 'Encrypted message' — never previews content

passphrase_screen.center_element: Large lemon slice logo, segments illuminate as passphrase strength increases

input.style: minimal underline-only input, lemon-yellow on focus

passphrase_screen.strength_indicator: Lemon slice fills segment by segment

key_fingerprint.format: Groups of 4 hex chars separated by spaces

splash.center: Lemon slice logo — segments animate in clockwise, then pulse once
splash.wordmark: SUBLEMONABLE in Clash Display 700, lemon yellow, tracked wide

slides.visual: Animated lemon slice — segments = encryption layers
slides.headline: End-to-end encrypted
slides.body: Your messages are locked before they leave your device. We can't read them. Nobody can.

slides.headline: Messages that disappear
slides.body: Set any message to self-destruct. Once read, once seen, then gone.

slides.headline: Screenshots? Blocked.
slides.body: On Android, screenshots are impossible. On iOS and browser, we blur instantly.

slides.headline: No phone number needed
slides.body: Add contacts by QR code or link. We don't need your number, your email, or your name.

chat_list.header: SUBLEMONABLE wordmark left-aligned, settings icon right
chat_list.fab: Compose button — lemon yellow circle, bottom right, lemon-glow shadow

header.actions: verify key (lemon slice icon), burn all (flame icon), back

chat_view.encryption_notice: Persistent micro-badge at top: '🔒 End-to-end encrypted' in muted lemon

settings.style: Dark grouped list, lemon accents on active toggles
sections: Privacy — default TTL, burn-on-read default, screenshot watermark
Network — Tor routing toggle, connection status

animation_moments.message_send: Bubble scales from compose bar with spring easing, slides up
animation_moments.message_burn: Flame particles emit upward from bubble, bubble chars and shrinks to a lemon-slice ash icon, then fades
animation_moments.encryption_handshake: Lemon slice segments illuminate one by one on first message to new contact
animation_moments.screenshot_blur: Frosted glass effect slides over message list in 120ms — fast and jarring on purpose
animation_moments.key_verified: Lemon slice fully illuminates in green with a single ring pulse
animation_moments.app_unlock: Biometric success: lemon slice opens like a iris, reveals chat list

brand_voice.tone: Dry, sharp, confident. Never corporate. Never paranoid. Security is effortless here.

good: Encrypted before it leaves your hands.
Screenshots get blurred. Snoops get nothing.

sections.visual: Animated lemon slice — large, centered, slowly rotating, casting lemon-yellow glow on dark background
sections.headline: Nothing lasts.
sections.body: Sublemonable is end-to-end encrypted messaging that disappears. No logs. No screenshots. No trace.

items.headline: Encrypted before it leaves your hands.
items.body: Signal Protocol. Every message gets its own key. We store nothing readable. Not because the law says so — because it's technically impossible.
items.visual: Animated key exchange diagram — two lemon slices passing encrypted segments between them

items.headline: Read it. Watch it go.
items.body: Burn-on-read. Timed destruction. 30 seconds to a week — your call. The server deletes the moment your recipient gets it.

items.headline: Screenshots? We blur those.
items.body: Android blocks them entirely. iOS and browser blur your messages the instant a screenshot is attempted. We also watermark every conversation — if something leaks, we know who did it.
items.visual: Split mockup: left phone blurred, right shows 'Screenshot blocked'

items.headline: No number. No email. No name.
items.body: Connect by QR code. Your identity is a key pair we generate on your device. We don't know who you are.
items.visual: QR code scan animation with lemon-slice overlay

steps.label: Generate keys
steps.description: Your device creates your identity. Keys never leave your device.

steps.label: Exchange codes
steps.description: Share a QR or link. No personal info changes hands.

steps.label: Send encrypted
steps.description: Every message encrypted locally before sending.

steps.label: Delivered and deleted
steps.description: Server purges message the instant it's delivered.

steps.label: Gone forever
steps.description: Set to burn, or burn on read. Nothing stays.

sections.headline: We show our work.
sections.body: Sublemonable is open source. The encryption, the server, the apps — all of it auditable. Trust shouldn't require faith.

download.content: App store badges + PWA install prompt + self-host instructions

visual_motifs.lemon_slice_hero: Large SVG lemon slice, slow ambient rotation, yellow glow on dark background
visual_motifs.particle_field: Subtle floating lemon-yellow particles on dark bg — like citrus oils in light
visual_motifs.message_preview: Animated mockup phones showing messages burning
visual_motifs.code_reveal: TypeScript/Go encryption code snippet with syntax highlighting in lemon theme

fable_ui_instructions.design_tokens_file: packages/ui/src/tokens.ts — export all color, type, spacing tokens
fable_ui_instructions.tailwind_config: Extend Tailwind with all brand tokens as custom properties
fable_ui_instructions.dark_mode: Dark only — no light mode in v1
fable_ui_instructions.font_loading: Preload Clash Display + Inter + JetBrains Mono via <link rel=preload>
fable_ui_instructions.lemon_slice_svg: Build as reusable React component with animated segments prop (0-8 filled)
critical_rules: NEVER use white backgrounds — minimum is #1A1800
NEVER use blue for any interactive element — lemon yellow owns interactivity
Message content is ALWAYS dark bg (#242100) with light text or lemon bg with dark text — never inverted
The burn animation must feel satisfying — not a simple opacity fade
Screenshot blur must be jarring and instant — 120ms max — it should feel protective
Every loading state uses the lemon slice spinner
Key fingerprints always render in JetBrains Mono
Compose bar send button is always lemon yellow — it is the primary action color

project.name: Sublemonable
project.description: The complete security onion. Every layer assumes the one beneath it has already failed. v1.5 adds plausible deniability, dead drop mode, decoy traffic, multi-hop relay, and Tor-first architecture — all before the blockchain layer of v2.
project.philosophy: Security is not a feature. It is an onion. Layer 4 failing must not expose layer 5. Layer 2 failing must not expose layer 4. Every layer is designed as if all others have already been compromised.

security_onion.description: Complete layered security model as of v1.5. Each layer is independent.

layer_1_physical.name: Physical Access
features: Panic wipe — 3-finger long press, full wipe in under 2 seconds
Duress PIN — alternate PIN that silently wipes while appearing to unlock
Plausible deniability — two vaults, one app, no evidence second vault exists
FLAG_SECURE — OS-level screenshot block on Android
Biometric lock — Face ID / Touch ID / fingerprint
Background app blur — content hidden when app not in foreground

layer_2_network.name: Network
features: TLS 1.3 — all transport encrypted
Certificate pinning — MITM impossible
Tor-first — default connection via Tor, clearnet is fallback not default
Multi-hop relay — 3-hop onion routing through relay network
Decoy traffic — continuous cover traffic, active and idle indistinguishable
obfs4 transport — Tor traffic obfuscated, looks like generic HTTPS

layer_3_identity.name: Identity
features: No phone number required
No email required
UUID routing — never exposed to other parties
Sealed Sender — relay network never knows sender identity
Dead drop mode — no direct communication channel between parties
QR-only contact exchange — no central discovery

layer_4_message.name: Message
features: Signal Protocol — E2EE with forward secrecy per message
Double Ratchet — new key every message
256-byte padding — message size analysis impossible
Burn on read — destruction on first open
TTL destruction — server purges on schedule regardless
Zero server logs — nothing stored to subpoena

layer_5_storage.name: Storage
features: Argon2id vault derivation — identical timing for all passphrases
Plausible deniability vaults — AES-256-GCM, cryptographically separate
AES-256-GCM at rest — all local data encrypted
Secure Enclave / Android Keystore — hardware-backed key operations
Memory zeroing — key material cleared from RAM immediately after use
Secure delete — overwrite before free on all sensitive data

connection_modes.description: Three user-selectable connection modes. Selectable in Settings → Network → Connection Mode.

standard.name: Standard
standard.description: Tor routing + single relay hop + Sealed Sender. Suitable for everyday use.
standard.indicator: Single lemon slice segment lit in settings

stealth.name: Stealth
stealth.description: Tor routing + 3-hop onion relay + decoy traffic. For sensitive communications.
stealth.indicator: Three lemon slice segments lit in settings
stealth.latency_warning: Messages may take 2-4 seconds longer to deliver

ghost.name: Ghost
ghost.description: Tor + 3-hop relay + continuous decoy traffic + dead drop only. No persistent identity visible on the network.
ghost.indicator: All lemon slice segments lit, pulsing slowly
ghost.latency_warning: Dead drop pickup requires recipient to be online or check manually
ghost.note: In Ghost mode, all messages are dead drops. No direct channel exists.

plausible_deniability.description: Two completely separate encrypted vaults behind two different passphrases. No cryptographic evidence the second vault exists.
plausible_deniability.principle: Both vaults are real registered accounts that can send and receive real messages. The decoy is not fake. It is a real identity.

vault_architecture.vault_count: Never stored anywhere. App tries passphrase against key slots. If it decrypts, that is the active profile.
vault_architecture.key_derivation: Argon2id with identical parameters for all passphrases — memory: 65536 KB, iterations: 3, parallelism: 4
vault_architecture.timing_attack_prevention: Both passphrases take identical wall-clock time to derive. No shortcut on decoy path. A stopwatch cannot distinguish them.
vault_architecture.storage_isolation: Vault A and Vault B use completely separate encrypted storage namespaces. No shared tables, no shared files.
vault_architecture.storage_padding: Decoy vault storage padded with random encrypted blocks proportional to total app storage — prevents size anomaly detection
vault_architecture.key_slots: App maintains 2 key slots (expandable to 4 in future). Each slot is a 32-byte random salt + AES-256-GCM encrypted vault key. Trying a passphrase attempts decryption of all slots. First successful decryption wins.
vault_architecture.no_vault_index: The integer number of vaults is never stored. Slot decryption failure is indistinguishable from wrong passphrase.
vault_architecture.server_accounts: Each vault has its own independent server account, identity key, and prekey bundle. The server cannot link them.
vault_architecture.notifications: Push notifications delivered as encrypted signals only. When decoy vault is active, real vault notifications are silenced and queued encrypted. No notification reveals real vault existence.

forensic_protections.file_timestamps: All vault files use normalized timestamps. OS-level file metadata does not reveal activity patterns.
forensic_protections.sqlite_wal: WAL journal files encrypted and wiped immediately on checkpoint. No recoverable fragments.
forensic_protections.indexeddb_web: All IndexedDB entries are encrypted blobs keyed by derived identifiers. Schema reveals nothing about vault count or content.
forensic_protections.secure_delete: All deleted content overwritten with random bytes before deallocation on all platforms.
forensic_protections.memory_forensics: Decrypted vault contents never written to disk. In-memory only. Zeroed immediately on app background.

decoy_best_practices.activity_reminder: App gently prompts if decoy profile unused for 7+ days: 'Your other profile has been quiet.' No explanation of why.
decoy_best_practices.realistic_contacts: Onboarding suggests adding 2-3 real public-facing contacts to decoy profile
decoy_best_practices.ui_identical: Decoy and real vault are visually identical. Same features, same performance, same everything.
decoy_best_practices.naming: Never called hidden vault, secret profile, or deniability in UI. Setup flow says only: 'Add a second passphrase for an alternate profile.'

platform_implementation.web: Custom encrypted storage layer over IndexedDB. All keys derived from vault key. No plaintext schema.
platform_implementation.ios: Separate Keychain entries per vault, keyed by derived fingerprint not vault index. Separate SQLite databases, both encrypted via SQLCipher.
platform_implementation.android: Separate EncryptedSharedPreferences namespaces. Separate encrypted SQLite via SQLCipher. Keystore entries keyed by derived alias.

plausible_deniability.legal_note: User provides passphrase A under compulsion. Decoy profile opens. No cryptographic evidence passphrase B exists. User has not provided false information. This mirrors the VeraCrypt hidden volume legal model.

dead_drop_mode.description: Asynchronous anonymous message deposit. No direct communication channel between sender and recipient. No metadata linking the two parties.
dead_drop_mode.tradecraft_origin: Spy tradecraft technique. Sender leaves message at agreed location. Recipient retrieves it independently. The two never communicate directly.

how_it_works.step_1: Sender creates a dead drop — generates a one-time random drop ID and access token
how_it_works.step_2: Sender encrypts message with recipient's public key (X3DH as normal)
how_it_works.step_3: Sender deposits encrypted envelope at a relay node under the drop ID
how_it_works.step_4: Drop ID and access token shared out-of-band — QR code, separate channel, in-person
how_it_works.step_5: Recipient redeems token at any relay node — no account required for pickup
how_it_works.step_6: Relay verifies token, delivers envelope, immediately destroys drop
how_it_works.step_7: Token is single-use. Replay attack returns empty.
how_it_works.step_8: No record of who deposited or who retrieved exists on any relay

anonymity_properties.sender_identity: Relay never knows. Drop deposited via Tor with no auth.
anonymity_properties.recipient_identity: Relay never knows. Token redemption requires no account.
anonymity_properties.communication_link: No metadata linking sender to recipient exists anywhere.
anonymity_properties.timing: Sender deposits and recipient retrieves at different times. No timing correlation.
anonymity_properties.token_format: 256-bit random token. Single use. HMAC-verified by relay.
anonymity_properties.after_ttl: Drop destroyed whether collected or not. Sender not notified — by design.

ui_flow.create_drop: Compose message → long press send → 'Send as dead drop' → generates QR code containing drop token → share QR out-of-band
ui_flow.redeem_drop: Main screen → '+' → 'Redeem dead drop' → scan QR → message delivered and drop destroyed
ui_flow.ghost_mode: In Ghost connection mode, all messages are automatically dead drops. No direct channels.

server_changes.new_endpoint: POST /api/v1/drops — deposit encrypted envelope + token hash (no auth required, proof-of-work instead)
server_changes.redeem_endpoint: POST /api/v1/drops/redeem — present token, receive envelope, drop destroyed
server_changes.storage: Drop table: id (hash of token), ciphertext (blob), expires_at. Nothing else.
server_changes.no_sender_field: Drop table has no sender column. By design. Server cannot know.
server_changes.proof_of_work: Hashcash puzzle required to deposit — prevents spam without requiring account

decoy_traffic.description: Continuous cover traffic that makes real message sending indistinguishable from idle state. Eliminates traffic analysis.
decoy_traffic.problem_solved: Even with Sealed Sender and Tor, an observer watching network traffic can see: this device sent something at 2:14am. Decoy traffic makes that observation meaningless.

implementation.mechanism: Background service generates fake encrypted envelopes at randomized intervals and submits them to relay nodes
implementation.indistinguishable: Decoy envelopes are padded to identical 256-byte block sizes as real messages. Same Tor circuit. Same relay nodes. Same submission path.
implementation.fake_recipients: Decoy traffic addressed to randomly generated UUIDs that resolve to nowhere. Relay stores briefly, TTL expires, purged. No real delivery.
implementation.timing_randomization: Poisson process interval distribution — not uniform, not predictable. Matches human messaging patterns.

intensity_levels.off: No decoy traffic. Default in Standard mode.
intensity_levels.low: 1 decoy per real message sent. Minimal battery impact.
intensity_levels.medium: Continuous at ~1 per 30-120 seconds randomized. Stealth mode default.
intensity_levels.high: Continuous at ~1 per 5-30 seconds randomized. Ghost mode default.

implementation.user_control: Settings → Privacy → Cover Traffic → Off / Low / Medium / High. Current mode shown as lemon slice fill level.

platform_notes.ios: BGProcessingTask for background decoy generation when app is backgrounded. Degrades gracefully if iOS kills background task.
platform_notes.android: WorkManager periodic task. Foreground service option for maximum reliability.
platform_notes.web: Service Worker background sync. Decoy traffic continues while browser tab is backgrounded.

multi_hop_relay.description: Onion-routed message delivery through a chain of 3 relay nodes. No single node knows both sender and recipient.
multi_hop_relay.inspired_by: Tor's onion routing — adapted for the Sublemonable relay network specifically.

how_it_works.path_selection: Client queries relay registry and selects 3 relay nodes randomly. Nodes in different geographic regions preferred.

onion_encryption.step_1: Client encrypts message for Relay C (innermost layer)
onion_encryption.step_2: Client wraps in encryption for Relay B
onion_encryption.step_3: Client wraps in encryption for Relay A (outermost layer)

how_it_works.relay_a: Knows sender IP (via Tor, so not real IP). Decrypts outer layer. Sees only: forward to Relay B. Does not know final recipient.
how_it_works.relay_b: Knows Relay A. Decrypts middle layer. Sees only: forward to Relay C. Knows neither sender nor recipient.
how_it_works.relay_c: Knows Relay B. Decrypts inner layer. Sees only: recipient ID + encrypted envelope. Does not know sender.
how_it_works.recipient: Picks up from Relay C. Decrypts with Signal Protocol. Sealed Sender reveals true sender only to recipient.

relay_a: Previous hop (Tor exit node — not real sender IP)

multi_hop_relay.compromise_resistance: An adversary must compromise all 3 relay nodes AND correlate timing to link sender and recipient. With Tor + decoy traffic this is computationally and practically infeasible.

relay_node_changes.new_capability: Relay nodes must support onion forwarding protocol — new in v1.5
relay_node_changes.forward_endpoint: POST /relay/forward — accepts onion-encrypted packet, decrypts one layer, forwards to next hop
relay_node_changes.no_logging: Forward operations never logged. Previous hop address zeroed from memory after forwarding.
relay_node_changes.version_flag: Relay nodes advertise multi-hop support in relay registry. Clients only use nodes that support it for multi-hop paths.

circuit_construction.path_diversity: No two hops in the same AS (Autonomous System) where possible
circuit_construction.path_rotation: New circuit constructed every 10 minutes or 100 messages, whichever comes first
circuit_construction.guard_nodes: First hop (guard) rotated less frequently — weekly — to reduce path discovery attacks

tor_first_architecture.description: Tor is the default connection method. Clearnet is the fallback with a visible warning indicator.

v1_vs_v15.v1_5: Default Tor. Clearnet is fallback only, shown with warning indicator in UI.

implementation.web: Connect via .onion address by default when accessed through Tor Browser. Detect Tor Browser and adjust automatically.
implementation.ios: Integrate Tor.framework (Guardian Project) — runs Tor in-process, no Orbot dependency
implementation.android: Embed Tor via NetCipher + Tor Android library — runs in-process, no Orbot dependency
implementation.fallback_indicator: Connection mode badge in chat header: green lemon slice = Tor active, yellow = clearnet fallback, red = no connection

onion_service.note: v3 onion addresses only — v2 deprecated and insecure
onion_service.self_hosted: Server operator configures .onion address in server config. Published in app and docs.
onion_service.onion_address_distribution: Hardcoded in app binary + published in SECURITY_MODEL.md + verifiable via signed announcement

no_orbot_dependency.rationale: Requiring Orbot creates a dependency the user must install separately. In-process Tor means Sublemonable works out of the box with Tor protection even if user has never heard of Orbot.
no_orbot_dependency.ios_library: Tor.framework by Guardian Project — battle tested, used by Onion Browser
no_orbot_dependency.android_library: tor-android by Guardian Project — same lineage

v1_to_v15_migration.user_impact: Existing v1 users see a one-time update prompt. All existing messages and contacts carry over. New features opt-in except Tor-first which is the new default.
v1_to_v15_migration.tor_default_rollout: Tor-first shown as new default with brief explanation on first launch after update. User can switch to clearnet if Tor is blocked in their region.
v1_to_v15_migration.second_passphrase_setup: Optional. Prompted once during update with explanation: 'Add a second passphrase to create an alternate profile.' No pressure, fully skippable.
v1_to_v15_migration.existing_accounts: Unchanged. v1.5 is fully backwards compatible with v1 server.

new: Multi-hop relay forwarding — POST /relay/forward on relay nodes
Relay capability advertisement — version flag in relay registry

phase_1.name: Storage Layer — Plausible Deniability
phase_1.why_first: Everything else builds on top of the storage layer. Get this right before building features that depend on it.
deliverables: Key slot architecture in packages/crypto — tryPassphrase(passphrase) -> VaultKey | null
Identical Argon2id timing for all passphrases — timing attack test included
Separate encrypted storage namespaces per vault on all platforms
Storage padding to normalize app storage size
Forensic protections — timestamp normalization, WAL encryption, secure delete
Second passphrase setup flow in all three apps
Notification silencing when decoy vault active
Unit tests: vault isolation, timing parity, slot count non-disclosure

phase_2.name: Tor-First Network
deliverables: Connection mode badge in chat header UI
v3 onion address configuration in server
Tor bootstrap progress shown during first connect (lemon slice fills as Tor connects)

phase_3.name: Dead Drop Mode
deliverables: Dead drop server endpoints — deposit and redeem
QR code generation for drop token sharing
QR code scanning for drop redemption
Dead drop UI flow in all three apps
Ghost mode — all messages as dead drops
Integration tests: deposit, redeem, replay attack rejection, TTL expiry

phase_4.name: Decoy Traffic
deliverables: Background decoy envelope generator in packages/relay-client
Poisson interval distribution for timing randomization
Battery impact monitoring and auto-reduce on low battery
Cover traffic intensity selector in settings UI (lemon slice fill level)
Verification: decoy envelopes indistinguishable from real in network capture

phase_5.name: Multi-Hop Relay
deliverables: Onion encryption layer in packages/crypto — 3-layer encrypt/decrypt
Path selection algorithm — geographic diversity, AS diversity
Multi-hop path indicator in settings UI
Integration tests: 3-hop delivery, single node compromise simulation
Performance tests: latency under multi-hop vs direct

phase_6.name: Ghost Mode Integration + Polish
deliverables: Ghost mode — ties all features together as one setting
Connection mode selector UI — Standard / Stealth / Ghost
Mode indicator in chat header — lemon slice segments show active layers
Decoy activity health check — warns if background tasks killed by OS
Full end-to-end Ghost mode test — verify no metadata leaks
Update docs/SECURITY_MODEL.md with full v1.5 onion diagram

connection_mode_badge.location: Chat header, right of contact name
connection_mode_badge.standard: Single lemon slice segment, green glow
connection_mode_badge.ghost: All segments, slow pulse, white glow
connection_mode_badge.clearnet_fallback: Yellow warning dot, tooltip: 'Tor unavailable — using direct connection'

second_passphrase_setup.label: Add a second passphrase
second_passphrase_setup.confirmation: Both passphrases confirmed, second vault initialized silently

dead_drop_ui.create: Long-press send button → 'Send as dead drop' option appears
dead_drop_ui.qr_screen: Full screen QR with lemon slice watermark, share sheet
dead_drop_ui.redeem: Main screen → compose button long-press → 'Redeem dead drop' → camera scan

cover_traffic_indicator.visual: Lemon slice with fill level showing intensity — 0 segments off, 3 segments medium, all 8 high

tor_bootstrap.first_launch: Lemon slice animates segment by segment as Tor bootstrap completes (0-100%)
tor_bootstrap.subsequent_launches: Silent — connects in background, badge updates when ready

fable_instructions.note: v1.5 blueprint — build AFTER v1.0 is merged and stable.
fable_instructions.start_here: Read versions.1.0.0 and this section completely before writing a single line of code. v1.5 is additive — do not modify existing working features.
order: 1. Build key slot architecture and vault isolation in packages/crypto — timing parity tests mandatory
2. Add second vault setup flow and storage padding to all three apps and web
3. Add forensic protections — timestamp normalization, WAL encryption, secure delete on all platforms
4. Embed Tor.framework in iOS and tor-android in Android — in-process, no Orbot dependency
5. Implement Tor-first connection with clearnet fallback and connection mode badge
6. Add v3 onion address to server configuration
7. Build dead drop server endpoints with proof-of-work deposit
8. Build dead drop client flow — create, QR share, redeem — in all three apps
9. Build decoy traffic generator with Poisson timing in packages/relay-client
10. Integrate decoy traffic background tasks on iOS, Android, and web
11. Build onion encryption layer (3-layer) in packages/crypto
12. Build circuit construction and path selection in packages/relay-client
13. Add relay forward endpoint to server and relay nodes
14. Build Ghost mode — ties all features together with single setting
15. Build connection mode selector UI — Standard / Stealth / Ghost with lemon slice indicators
16. Full end-to-end Ghost mode integration test
17. Update SECURITY_MODEL.md with complete v1.5 onion diagram
18. Update CHANGELOG.md and tag v1.5.0
critical_rules: PLAUSIBLE DENIABILITY IS ONLY AS STRONG AS ITS WEAKEST IMPLEMENTATION — timing parity is non-negotiable
NEVER store the number of vaults anywhere — slot count must be unknowable
NEVER label second vault as hidden, secret, or deniable anywhere in UI or code comments visible to forensics
NEVER allow notification content to reveal real vault existence when decoy is active
DEAD DROP relay endpoint requires NO authentication — proof-of-work only — account auth would defeat anonymity
DECOY envelopes must be byte-for-byte indistinguishable from real envelopes — same padding, same Tor circuit, same relay nodes
TOR is now default — clearnet is fallback — never present clearnet as the primary option
MULTI-HOP path selection must never place two hops in the same AS — defeats the purpose
GHOST MODE must be tested with network capture to verify no metadata leaks before release
ALL v1 critical rules still apply — no logging, no plaintext, parameterized SQL, JWT validation etc
ARGON2ID parameters must be identical for all vault passphrases — no optimization shortcut on decoy path
MEMORY zeroing applies doubly in v1.5 — vault keys especially must never linger in RAM
testing_requirements: Timing attack test: 1000 passphrase attempts on vault A and vault B — mean and stddev must be statistically indistinguishable
Forensic test: after using real vault, switch to decoy — no file artifacts, no timestamps, no recoverable fragments
Network capture test: 60 seconds of Ghost mode traffic — real message send must be indistinguishable from decoy traffic
Multi-hop test: simulate Relay B compromise — verify sender and recipient remain unknown to Relay B
Dead drop replay test: redeeming same token twice must return 404 on second attempt
Notification test: receive message on real vault while decoy active — no visible notification reveals real vault
